translator: Swami Gambhirananda (per Advaita Ashrama edition; bhagavad-gita.us reproduces this English rendering of Sankara-bhasya)
verbatim_excerpt: 2.47 Te, your; adhikarah, right; is karmani eva, for action alone, not for steadfastness in Knowledge. Even there, when you are engaged in action, you have ma kadacana, never; a right phalesu, for the results of action — may you not have a hankering for the results of action.
copyright_holder: Advaita Ashrama, Kolkata (publisher of Swami Gambhirananda's English translation of Sankara-bhasya). Site bhagavad-gita.us reproduces the translation; we cite a short fair-use excerpt only.
notes: Page renders all sampradaya commentaries in static HTML (the JS only toggles visibility), so static fetch with browser User-Agent works; no Playwright needed. Full commentary length 858 chars; excerpt is the opening 276 chars covering the core gloss of adhikāraḥ, karmaṇi, and phaleṣu. Sanskrit transliteration in the source uses non-IAST 'adhikarah' / 'phalesu' / 'kadacana' — preserved verbatim.
